Expected: 2000, Got: 2000

Expected: 2000, Got: 2000
=== Static Synchronization ===
Static counter: 2000
phase1.explanation:
Synchronization prevents race conditions:

Race Condition:
• Multiple threads access shared data simultaneously
• Results are inconsistent and unpredictable
• Example: counter++ with two threads

Synchronized Method:
• Only one thread can execute the method at a time
• Locks on 'this' object
• synchronized void method() { ... }

Synchronized Block:
• More granular than synchronized methods
• Locks on specific object (can be 'this' or custom lock)
• synchronized (lock) { ... }

Static Synchronization:
• Locks on the Class object
• static synchronized void method() { ... }

Why synchronize?
• Prevents data corruption
• Ensures consistent results
• Thread-safe code

Performance impact:
• Synchronization has overhead
• Use only when needed

Atomic Operations:
• Some operations are atomic (assignment to primitives)
• Others are not (value++ is 3 operations: read, add, write)

⚠️ Deadlocks: Threads waiting for each other's locks

⚠️ Starvation: Threads never get access to lock

phase2.errorMessage:
Counter: 154623

(No error — but the result is WRONG! This is a race condition.)

phase3.prompt:
Create a BankAccount class with:
• Private balance (double)
• Synchronized deposit(double amount) method
• Synchronized withdraw(double amount) method
• Synchronized getBalance() method

In main:
1. Create a BankAccount with $1000
2. Create 5 threads that each deposit $100 (500 total)
3. Create 3 threads that each withdraw $50 (150 total)
4. All threads run simultaneously
5. Print final balance (should be $1350 if correct)
dialogueHints: All three methods should be synchronized
Use join() to wait for all threads to finish
